fair_value_today.basis: base case (Metis) intrinsic value — the anchor, not a weighted blend

fair_value_12m.basis: #1 rolled forward at r=11.0% less dividend 0.0

detail.multiple_rationale: Comparable: Innoviz (INVZ) currently trades at 15-18x NTM EV/Sales with production programs underway. AEVA should trade at a modest premium (18-19x) to Innoviz given the larger and exclusive EU OEM program. But the current 45x+ NTM EV/Sales for AEVA is unsustainable as the ramp story matures into an execution story. 18.8x is the re-rating level when first production is live. This credits both contracted programs but NOT the extrapolated Luminar replacement wins.

conviction.label: LOW
conviction.basis: action SELL: Daedalus assigned LOW (evidence read across base/bull/bear) -> LOW

sensitivity_matrix.description: Two dominant levers: (1) NOPAT at normalization (driven by revenue × margin — the crux) and (2) WACC (the discount rate). ROIC is second-order (a 5pp change moves fair value ~$0.50). The matrix shows base case fair value ($/share) at each cell.
sensitivity_matrix.dominant_lever_note: WACC is the dominant lever. Moving from 11% to 9% with base-case NOPAT takes fair value from $5.05 to $13.70 — a 171% increase on a 2pp WACC change. This is WHY the market (using ~9% WACC) sees the stock at $24-25 while our fundamental WACC of 11% gives $5. The WACC disagreement is the debate, not the revenue trajectory.

street_reconstruction.implied_wacc_at_base_nopat: ~9% (the $89M base NOPAT at 9% WACC ≈ $13.70; the $25 street target at 9% WACC requires NOPAT_T ~$167M, i.e. the bull case). The street is pricing the bull case revenue at a growth-investor WACC. The question is whether 9% is the right discount rate for this company.
street_reconstruction.street_thesis_decoded: Street consensus ($25 mean, strong buy): (1) FMCW wins the technical race for L3/L4 autonomy; (2) contracted programs plus Luminar clearing drive 3-4 OEM production wins by 2028; (3) revenue reaches $300-500M by 2030-2032; (4) market should value at 9-10% WACC given contracted program visibility. Our disagreement: the WACC is too low for a pre-profit hardware company; the Luminar replacement wins are extrapolated, not contracted.
street_reconstruction.our_disagreement: WACC: we use 11% vs street implied ~9%. Revenue ramp: our base ($290M FY2033) is below the street bull case; we do not credit the full Luminar replacement scenario. The street's $25 target is the bull case, not the base case.

bridge_logic: #1 fair value today 5.05 -> #3 fair value 12m 5.61 (x(1+0.110) - div 0.0); #4 target 12m 14.0 (defensible_multiple); implied return to #4 = -42.6%, to #3 = -77.0%, to street mean None = None%
